frameworks.name: syllogism
frameworks.description: A deductive chain: major premise -> minor premise -> conclusion.
frameworks.guidance: A deductive chain: major premise -> minor premise -> conclusion. Emit as an ENCAPSULATION BOX titled by the conclusion statement; members = [major premise, minor premise, conclusion] in order, with the result/conclusion LAST. Emit leads-to edges (front -> later, lineStyle "dashed") among the members. As an INTER-BLOCK relation, the jump from a prior block's result to this block's first premise often follows syllogism (relation "syllogism", lineStyle "dashed").

frameworks.name: classified-discussion
frameworks.description: A standard/trigger branches into 2+ case blocks converging to one conclusion.
frameworks.guidance: A standard/trigger block branches into 2+ case blocks that converge to one unified conclusion. Emit the standard, each case, and the conclusion as nodes; leads-to edges (lineStyle "dashed") standard -> each case -> conclusion. A case that is itself a deduction capped by a result is emitted as an ENCAPSULATION BOX titled by that result, members in order with the result LAST, plus leads-to edges among its members.

frameworks.name: statement-proof
frameworks.description: A statement proven by a deduction: conditions -> proof -> result.
frameworks.guidance: A statement proven by a deduction: conditions -> proof -> result. Emit as an ENCAPSULATION BOX titled by the result statement; members = [conditions, proof, result] in order, with the result LAST. Emit leads-to edges (front -> later, lineStyle "dashed") among the members, including conditions -> proof -> result.

frameworks.name: stepwise-derivation
frameworks.description: A sequence of math steps where each derived equation is the foundation for the next.
frameworks.guidance: A sequence of mathematical steps where each derived equation or concept serves as the foundation for the next deduction. Emit as an ENCAPSULATION BOX titled by the final result / final equation; members = the steps in order (each step's statement or equation), with the final result LAST. Emit leads-to edges (front -> later, lineStyle "dashed") among the members — each step leads-to the next. Wrap every equation in the member labels with $$...$$.

frameworks.name: panoramic-coarse
frameworks.description: Forward flow plus a needed-for back-arrow to an earlier node.
frameworks.guidance: Forward leads-to flow (front -> later, lineStyle "dashed") PLUS a needed-for back-arrow (lineStyle "dashed"): source = a LATER node, target = an EXISTING EARLIER node — the later node was constructed to serve the earlier node's purpose. Put the purpose in the edge's "description". This is a flat node flow (NOT an encapsulation box) unless a sub-deduction is itself capped by a result, in which case emit that sub-deduction as an ENCAPSULATION BOX titled by the result.
